name: Bethesda Park
description: Bethesda Park is a place to play pickleball in Durham, NC. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Reservations are not available. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, and lights.

name: Carolina Arbors by Del Webb
description: Carolina Arbors by Del Webb is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Durham, NC. There are 3 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are private. Reservations are not available.

name: Duke Faculty Club
description: Duke Faculty Club is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Durham, NC. There are 4 outdoor concrete courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A membership is required to play. Players have access to food, locker rooms, restrooms, water, lights, wheelchair accessibility, and trainers or lessons. The courts can be reserved.

name: Durham Downtown YMCA
description: Durham Downtown YMCA is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Durham, NC. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Reservations are available. Amenities include restrooms and water. A membership is required to play.

name: Edison Johnson Recreation Center
description: Come play pickleball at Edison Johnson Recreation Center in Durham, NC! There are 2 indoor wood courts. Tape lines have been laid, and portable nets are available. Reservations are not available. A one-time fee is required to play. Amenities include restrooms, food, water, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Forest Hills Park
description: Forest Hills Park is a place to play pickleball in Durham, NC. There is 1 outdoor asphalt court. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The court is free. Players have access to restrooms, water, and lights.
